items.front: Permitted uses of the term 'takeoff' per 4-3-1
items.back: Only to actually clear an aircraft for takeoff or to cancel a takeoff clearance.

items.front: Acceptable substitute terms for 'takeoff' in clearances
items.back: depart, departure, or fly

items.front: Clearance limit is an airport: required word after name
items.back: 'airport' must follow the airport name. Phraseology: CLEARED TO (destination) AIRPORT.

items.front: Clearance limit is a NAVAID: what follows name
items.back: The type of NAVAID must follow the NAVAID name. Phraseology: CLEARED TO (NAVAID name and type).

items.front: Clearance limit is an intersection/waypoint: what follows name
items.back: The type must follow the intersection or waypoint name. Phraseology: CLEARED TO (intersection/waypoint name and type).

items.front: AF1 destination handling in departure clearance
items.back: Do not specify the destination airport. Use 'DESTINATION AS FILED.' Presidential detail ensures accuracy.

items.front: Effect of vectoring an aircraft off a published SID or ODP
items.back: The vector cancels the DP. ATC becomes responsible for separation from terrain/obstructions. IFR aircraft must be assigned an altitude.

items.front: DVA heading assignment deadline
items.back: May be given as part of initial clearance but no later than with the takeoff clearance. Cannot interrupt climb in DVA until at or above MVA/MIA.

items.front: SID + DVA concurrent use
items.back: Not permitted. When a departure clearance includes a SID, concurrent use of a DVA is not permitted.

items.front: SID without published crossing restrictions: altitude instruction
items.back: Use MAINTAIN (altitude), NOT Climb via SID. Applies to Radar Vector SIDs and SIDs without published crossing restrictions.

items.front: Effect of 'MAINTAIN (altitude)' on prior altitude restrictions
items.back: Cancels previously issued altitude restrictions including SID/STAR restrictions, unless they are restated or modified.

items.front: Military/ALTRV/stereo routes: applicability of 4-3-2 procedures
items.back: Don't apply 4-3-2 procedures to military ALTRV, stereo routes, operations above FL 600, or other special handling military ops.

items.front: SID crossing altitude change phraseology
items.back: (SID name) DEPARTURE, EXCEPT CROSS (revised altitude information).

items.front: Non-towered airport in Class E surface area: turn/heading rule
items.back: Obtain/solicit the pilot's concurrence before issuing a turn or heading in a clearance.

items.front: Airports without ATC service or Class E surface area: turn rule
items.back: Do not specify direction of takeoff/turn. Issue initial heading so it applies only within controlled airspace.

items.front: FRC meaning and placement on flight strip
items.back: Full Route Clearance Necessary. Must always be the first item of intra-center remarks.

items.front: FRC/(fix) meaning
items.back: Full Route Clearance Necessary to the specified fix. If no fix is specified, full route clearance is required for the entire route.

items.front: Three conditions for abbreviated departure clearance (4-3-3a)
items.back: 1) Route not changed before departure. 2) All facilities have sufficient route info. 3) Destination airport info relayed prior to departure.

items.front: Pilot action when route changed but abbreviated clearance offered
items.back: Do not accept abbreviated clearance. Inform control facility on initial radio contact. Company/ops officer must inform pilot of changes.

items.front: Three mechanisms for non-towered airport departures (4-3-4)
items.back: 1) Departure release  2) Hold for release (HFR)  3) Release time

items.front: Hold for release (HFR) meaning
items.back: Departure clearance is not valid until additional instructions are received. Must include departure delay information.

items.front: EDCT departure window (non-CFR)
items.back: No earlier than 5 minutes before and no later than 5 minutes after the EDCT (-5/+5).

items.front: CFR (Call for Release) airborne window
items.back: Airborne within a window from 2 minutes prior to 1 minute after the assigned time (-2/+1).

items.front: Time check requirement for release/void times
items.back: Required when specifying an actual clock time. Not required when using a specified number of minutes.

items.front: Clearance void time expiry effect
items.back: Does NOT cancel clearance or IFR flight plan. Withdraws authority to depart IFR until new release/release time is issued and acknowledged.

items.front: Clearance void time pilot notification deadline
items.back: Pilot must advise ATC of intentions no later than 30 minutes after the clearance void time if not airborne.

items.front: Ground stop (GS) release rule
items.back: Do not release an aircraft subject to a GS without the approval of the GS originator.

items.front: Delay sequencing order for departure clearances
items.back: Issue clearances in the order requests were originally made, if practicable.

items.front: Forwarding departure delay information
items.back: Inform approach control facilities and/or towers of anticipated departure delays.

items.front: Pre-departure coordination threshold (without automation)
items.back: Less than 15 minutes flying time from transferring facility's boundary.

items.front: Pre-departure coordination threshold (with automatic data transfer)
items.back: May be reduced to 5 minutes or replaced with mileage from boundary parameter when mutually agreeable.

items.front: Forwarding departure time: assumed time tolerance
items.back: Unless assumed departure times are within 3 minutes of actual departure time, the actual time must be forwarded.

items.front: VFR release of IFR departure authorization phraseology
items.back: VFR DEPARTURE AUTHORIZED. CONTACT (facility) ON (frequency) AT (location or time if required) FOR CLEARANCE.

items.front: What pilot must be told during VFR release authorization
items.back: Proper frequency and, if appropriate, where or when to contact the facility for clearance.

items.front: Controller action when clearance facility cannot issue clearance for VFR departure
items.back: Suggest delay on ground. If pilot insists on VFR departure, inform facility holding flight plan of pilot's intentions and VFR departure time.

items.front: DM flashing in data block meaning
items.back: Unsuccessful transmission of a departure message.

items.front: Alternatives to forwarding departure times (4-3-10 note)
items.back: 1) LOA prescribing assumed departure times. 2) Mandatory radar handoff procedures.

items.front: 4-3-10 departure time forwarding: who receives them
items.back: 1) Facility from which clearance was received. 2) Terminal departure controller when involved in departure sequence.
